column2: 你認定 %s 再也沒用了，順手丟進垃圾桶，從此不見天日！

column1: You cleverly take nothing, and throw nothing away.
column2: 你聰明地什麼也沒拿，什麼也沒丟。

column1: You are on a fire escape. A rusty ladder is at one end. To the right of the fire escape another window waits, just barely out of your reach.
column2: 你站在逃生梯上。一端有座生鏽的梯子。逃生梯右邊還有一扇窗，剛好搆不到。

column1: Try moving to the west end of the fire escape.
column2: 試試移動到逃生梯的西端。

column1: There's no way to reach the ladder from down here. It's only accessible from above.
column2: 從下面根本碰不到梯子，只能從上面接近。

column1: You already ARE tied to the railing.
column2: 你已經被綁在欄杆上了啦。

column1: You'll be better off if you leave the ribbon exactly where it is.
column2: 那條緞帶還是別去動它比較好。

column1: Good idea. But first, tie the ribbon to yourself.
column2: 好主意。不過得先把緞帶綁在自己身上。

column1: It's not long enough to reach from here.
column2: 從這裡搆，長度不夠。

column1: (Of course, you've heard THAT line before!)
column2: （這句台詞你當然聽過，都聽到爛了！）

column1: Looking inside the left window, you see the room you just left.
column2: 望進左邊那扇窗，你看到自己剛離開的房間。

column1: There are two windows high above you. The left window near the fire escape has a dim yellow glow; the other window is dark. There may be something on the sill of the dark window, but you can't quite make it out from way down here.
column2: 你頭頂上有兩扇窗。逃生梯旁的左窗透著昏黃燈光；另一扇則是黑的。暗窗的窗台上好像擺著什麼，可惜你站在這麼下面根本看不清楚。

column1: You're currently all tied up!
column2: 你現在正被五花大綁著呢！

column1: There's just no way to reach the windows from here. They're only accessible from above.
column2: 從這裡根本搆不到那些窗，只能從上面進去。

column1: The window is already open. There's no use in breaking it!
column2: 窗戶早就開著了，你砸個什麼勁！

column1: There's no use throwing the hammer up there.
column2: 把槌子丟上去也沒用。

column1: (Besides, you probably just miss the window any way!)
column2: （反正你這種準頭，大概也丟不中窗戶！）

column1: Looking at the dark window, you notice a bottle of pills sitting on the window ledge.
column2: 盯著那扇暗窗看，你注意到窗台上擺著一瓶藥丸。

column1: There may be something in the east window, but from here, you're not sure.
column2: 東邊那扇窗好像有東西，不過從這兒看不準。

column1: You broke it, didn't you?
column2: 你打破了吧，是不是？

column1: There's no way to reach the window from here.
column2: 從這兒根本搆不到那扇窗。

column1: You consider sticking your hand through a solid glass window, but realize that if you did, you wouldn't be able to play the violin.
column2: 你考慮把手伸進厚實的玻璃窗，但轉念一想，這樣以後就沒辦法拉小提琴了。

column1: (Of course, you can't play the violin now, but you get the idea.)
column2: （雖然你現在根本不會拉小提琴，但意思你懂就好。）

column1: You already took the pills. Why go through all this again?
column2: 藥丸你早就拿走了，幹嘛還要重來一次？

column1: You already smashed the window. Why do it again?
column2: 窗戶你早就砸過了，何必再砸一次？

column1: You might try reaching over to the window first.
column2: 你可以先試著把手伸向那扇窗。

column1: You could never pry that window open with your pocket knife.
column2: 憑你那把小刀，休想撬開那扇窗。

column1: That's an idea; it's just not a good idea!
column2: 這是個主意，只不過是個爛主意！

column1: The fence is much too tall for you to climb over.
column2: 那道柵欄高到你翻不過去。

column1: Unfortunately the lid on the trash bin just will not close.
column2: 可惜垃圾桶的蓋子怎樣都蓋不上。

column1: Smart move, Larry!
column2: 高招啊，賴瑞！

column1: A sense of foreboding washes over your heart when your eyes slowly examine this dark alley that seems to be just outside every public area in Lost Wages. Is that just another kindly citizen or a vicious mugger quickly approaching you?
column2: 你的目光緩緩掃過這條暗巷——失樂園每個熱鬧地方外頭好像都藏著這麼一條——一股不祥的預感湧上心頭。朝你快步走來的，是好心市民，還是兇狠的搶匪？

column1: Larry, when are you gonna learn to stay out of these dark alleys!!
column2: 賴瑞，你到底什麼時候才學得會別亂鑽暗巷啦！！

column1: Hey, guys!! He's screwed up again! Dump this one down the tubes, and get him another body!
column2: 「嘿，各位！他又凸槌了！把這個塞進滑道，再給他換一具身體！」

column1: Pardon me, buddy, you say, "do you have any spare change?"
column2: 「麻煩一下，老兄，」你說，「有零錢嗎？」

column1: (He seems unamused!)
column2: （他一臉不爽！）

column1: Hey, you! you challenge, "come over here and fight like a man!"
column2: 「嘿，你！」你嗆聲，「有種過來像個男人一樣單挑！」

column1: (Dumb idea, Larry!)
column2: (爛主意，Larry！)

column1: Your attempt to frighten the mugger fails.
column2: 你想嚇唬搶匪，失敗了。

column1: There's only one thing he wants, Larry: everything!
column2: 他要的東西只有一樣，賴瑞：你的全部家當！

column1: (Now you know what we have to go through every time you press "Restart!")
column2: （現在你知道每次你按下「重新開始」，我們得經歷什麼了吧！）

column1: You wonder if cab companies have full-time employees whose sole job is to make these floors sticky.
column2: 你不禁懷疑，計程車行是不是專門請人把車廂地板弄得黏答答的。

column1: Sorry, Mac, but my meter's runnin'. Where didja wanna go?
column2: 「歹勢啦，老兄，錶一直在跳。你要去哪？」

column1: Waddaya tryin' to do, peahead! You're not gonna stiff me, and get away with it! Step outside, Deadbeat!!
column2: 「你他媽想幹嘛，死腦筋！想賴帳想得美！滾下車，賴皮鬼！！」

column1: Whew! You're glad to get out of here.
column2: 呼～能離開這裡，你真是鬆了口氣。

column1: So, wadda we gonna do, buddy? Ya just gonna sit here all night? Some guys gotta earn a livin', ya know!
column2: 「所以咧，老兄？你打算坐在這一整晚？有人可是要討生活的，你知道吧！」

column1: So? Ya gonna pay up? Or does I have ta get rough wit' cha??
column2: 「怎樣？你要付錢，還是要我對你來硬的？？」

column1: So? Ya gonna get outta my cab or not? Thanks for da business, but I didn't want ta make no career outta this!
column2: 「所以呢？你到底要不要下車？謝謝惠顧，不過我可不想靠這個吃一輩子！」

column1: The cabbie snarls a "Where to, buddy?" as he starts the meter rolling.
column2: 計程車司機邊發動計費表邊咆哮：「要去哪，老兄？」

column1: Well, here we are, buddy. That'll be $%d.00.
column2: 「好了，到囉，老兄。一共 $%d.00。」

column1: Thanks, buddy. Maybe I'll see ya again sometime!
column2: 「謝啦，老兄。說不定哪天還會再碰面！」

column1: The driver looks at you and says, "Hey! What's that you got in your coat there? Is that wine?"
column2: 司機瞄了你一眼說：「嘿！你外套裡藏的是什麼？紅酒？」

column1: Before you can move, the cabbie grabs your precious box of wine and guzzles it all down! Ohmigawd, Larry, hang on!!
column2: 你還沒反應過來，司機一把搶走你寶貝的紅酒，咕嚕咕嚕全灌下肚！天啊，賴瑞，抓緊了！！

column1: Brace yourself, Larry. He's swerving all over the road!
column2: 坐穩囉，賴瑞。他整台車在路上蛇行！

column1: It seems a bridge abutment has brought your evening in Lost Wages to a sudden, final, and complete halt!
column2: 一根橋墩，讓你在失樂園的夜晚戛然而止——突然、終結、乾脆俐落！

column1: No air bag, eh?
column2: 沒有安全氣囊喔？

column1: Remember, Larry: "Friends don't let cabbies drive drunk!"
column2: 記住了，賴瑞：「朋友不會讓計程車司機酒駕上路！」

column1: You wish you hadn't. Mr. America this guy ain't. His strongest feature is his aroma!
column2: 你後悔看了。這傢伙絕對稱不上型男，他最出眾的特色是他的體香！

column1: The cabbie wonders why you keep looking at him, but in the mirror he flashes you a coy little smile and a knowing wink.
column2: 司機納悶你幹嘛一直盯著他看，卻還是從後照鏡拋來一個嬌羞的微笑和心照不宣的眨眼。

column1: (Your stomach turns, and not from the ride!)
column2: （你反胃了，而且不是暈車害的！）

column1: You can't reach the cabbie from your seat. Why not talk to him?
column2: 你這位子搆不到司機。何不跟他聊聊天？

column1: Yuck! Your stomach turns at the thought.
column2: 噁！光用想的你就反胃了。

column1: This cab smells terrible!
column2: 這台車臭到有剩！

column1: We're there already; just sit tight, willya, Buddy?
column2: 「已經到了啦，坐好別亂動，行嗎，老兄？」

column1: You ask the cabbie, "Where's the action?"
column2: 你問司機：「哪裡有樂子？」

column1: Lookin' for action, eh? Dis town is really full of it. We gotta disco full of foxes this time of night. Den dere's the casino, next to the all-night weddin' chapel. And we gotta lovely bar, but you've already been dere. Me, I like da convenience store next to da disco, but of course, that's just personal preference.
column2: 「找樂子喔？這鎮上多得是。這時間迪斯可舞廳裡全是辣妹。再來是賭場，就在通宵婚禮教堂隔壁。還有間不錯的酒吧，不過你已經去過了。我自己是比較愛舞廳旁邊那間便利商店啦，當然，個人口味不同。」

column1: Ok. You got it, Mac!
column2: 「好啦，沒問題，老兄！」

column1: It won't take us long to get back dere, but I'll have to circle 'round da block.
column2: 「回那邊不用太久，不過我得繞一圈街區。」

column1: Uh, excuse me, Mr. Driver, you say, "but I think I may wish to change my mind about my destination this evening. Would that be all right with you?"
column2: 「呃，司機先生，」你說，「我想我今晚可能要改一下目的地。這樣可以嗎？」

column1: Geez, some guys jes' can't make up der minds!
column2: 「天啊，有些人就是沒個準主意！」

column1: Uh, excuse me, Mr. Driver, you say, "but I think I may wish to change my mind yet again about my destination this evening. Would that be too much trouble?"
column2: 「呃，司機先生，」你說，「我想我今晚可能又要再改一次目的地。這樣會太麻煩嗎？」

column1: Nah, ya changed your min' already! I ain' gonna spen' all night driving you in circles! Next time, figgur out where ya wanna go before ya gets in da cab!
column2: 「不行，你已經改過一次了！我才不要一整晚載你繞圈圈！下次上車前先想清楚要去哪！」

column1: Sheepishly, you apologize to the cabbie for not carrying enough money to pay for the ride.
column2: 你尷尬地向司機道歉，因為身上帶的錢不夠付車資。

column1: Waddaya mean, `no money!!' snarls the driver. "Step outsid'a ma office, chump!"
column2: 「你說什麼叫『沒錢！！』」司機咆哮道，「滾出我的地盤，白癡！」

column1: I appreciate your offer of a tip, but it's against my personal philosophy to accept gratuities!
column2: 「謝謝你要給我小費，不過收小費違反我的人生哲學！」

column1: Why don'cha jes' wait'll we git there, buddy?
column2: 「你就不能等到了再說嗎，老兄？」

column1: You quietly and carefully spritz a little spray around, vainly trying to kill some of this odor!
column2: 你悄悄小心噴了點香水，妄想能壓下這股臭味！

column1: Nah, fruit gives me gas! he grunts.
column2: 「不要，水果讓我脹氣！」他咕噥道。

column1: Hey, hurry up, you cry, "can't you see it's getting late?!"
column2: 「嘿，快點啦，」你喊道，「沒看到天色已經晚了嗎？！」

column1: Time waits for no man! he replies cryptically.
column2: 「歲月不等人啊！」他神祕兮兮地回了一句。

column1: I got no use for a diamond ring, shouts the cabbie, "especially one wit a stone like dat! Dat sucker sure looks fake to me!"
column2: 「鑽戒對我沒用，」司機喊道，「尤其是那種石頭！那玩意兒看起來假到爆！」

column1: No, thanks, buddy, replies the cabbie, "but have you got any wine?"
column2: 「不用了，老兄，」司機回道，「不過你有紅酒嗎？」

column1: What's that, a tricorder? he asks. "Don't tell me you're one of dem science fiction freaks?"
column2: 「那是啥，三度儀？」他問。「別跟我說你是那種科幻宅？」

column1: Well, ain't chu all growed up!
column2: 「喲，你可真是長大了啊！」

column1: Thanks, buddy, says the cabbie, as he grabs your box of candy and proceeds to gobble down every piece!
column2: 「謝啦，老兄，」司機說著搶過你的糖果盒，一顆接一顆狂嗑猛吃！

column1: (Bad move, Larry! You feel certain you're going to meet someone someday who would really enjoy a piece!)
column2: （衰啊，賴瑞！你篤定哪天會遇到一個正妹，兩人一起「呷一塊」——可惜糖現在全進了司機的肚子！）

column1: No, I don't want to go dancin' with you later, says the cabbie.
column2: 「不用，」司機說，「我等等沒興趣跟你去跳舞。」

column1: Not HIM again! Geez, does that guy get around. And I DO mean around!
column2: 「又是他！老天，這哥們兒還真『到處跑』。我說的是真的『到處跑』，你懂的！」

column1: The air in here IS thick enough to cut!
column2: 這裡的氣氛尷尬到能拿刀切開！

column1: No, thanks, says the cabbie, "I gotta subscription!"
column2: 「不用了，」司機說，「我有訂閱的啦！」

column1: Don't hit the cabbie's head with your hammer; you might hurt it.
column2: 別拿槌子敲司機的頭，你可能會傷到它。

column1: (The hammer, that is!)
column2: （槌子啦，不然你以為是什麼！）

column1: Hey, thanks! he cries. "One of those and I'll be stuck in fifth gear all night."
column2: 「嘿，謝啦！」他大喊。「吃一顆，我整晚都會卡在五檔沒法停！」

column1: (Good idea, Larry! After all you had to go through to get those pills!)
column2: （想得美，賴瑞！你費了九牛二虎之力才弄到那些藥丸耶！）

column1: Wat a coinci-dince! I wuz jes talkin' 'bout ribbon like dat with dis stacked babe fare I hadda little while ago. She wuz tellin' me 'bout how she tied up some dumb dweeb and took all his dough. Boy, did we have a good laugh at that one! You shouldda been here!!
column2: 「哇塞，這也太巧了！我剛才才跟一個身材超辣的客人聊到這種緞帶咧。她跟我說她怎麼把一個蠢蛋綁起來，把他身上的錢全部搜刮光。天啊，我們笑到不行！你剛剛要是在場就好了！！」

column1: (Somehow you feel you were!)
column2: （不知怎地，你有種「其實我就在場」的感覺！）

column1: Here, take this! you offer.
column2: 「來，拿去！」你遞了過去。

column1: Nah, I got no need for that! he replies.
column2: 「不用，」他回答，「我用不著那玩意兒！」

column1: You scratch your head in amazement at the way this cab can still operate with such severe rear wheel camber!
column2: 你搔著頭，佩服這台車後輪都歪成這樣了居然還能跑！

column1: Talkin' to yourself, eh? smirks the cabbie. "A guy in a job like this runs into lots o' weirdos!"
column2: 「跟自己講話喔？」司機賊笑。「幹我們這行的，什麼怪咖都見識過！」

column1: Leave yourself alone, Larry!
column2: 放過自己一下吧，賴瑞！

column1: It seems to be running a little fast, but then, this is an expensive town!
column2: 錶好像跳得有點快，不過話說回來，這可是個高消費的城市！

column1: You don't really want to steal his license plate!
column2: 你其實沒那麼想偷他的車牌啦！

column1: You don't have the key to the trunk.
column2: 你沒有後車廂的鑰匙。

column1: The numbers at the top of the video blackjack machine indicate the size of your current bet and the total amount of credit you have in the machine.
column2: 電子二十一點機上方的數字，顯示你目前的下注金額，以及機器裡剩下的總點數。

column1: For help, just look at the various buttons.
column2: 需要幫助的話，看看上面各個按鈕就知道了。

column1: Come on, dammit, pay off!
column2: 「來啊，該死的，快給我吐錢！」

column1: Click on a button instead of where you just clicked.
column2: 請點按鈕，不要再點你剛剛戳的地方。

column1: Screw you, machine! you shout, never one to be intimidated by mere machinery.
column2: 「去你的，臭機器！」你大罵，你才不會被一台機器嚇到。

column1: You gently kiss your machine to bring good luck.
column2: 你輕輕親了一下機器，祈求好運降臨。

column1: You carefully slip the pocketknife into the hole in the back of the machine, twist the odds screw, and hope no one saw you.
column2: 你小心翼翼把小刀插進機器後面的孔，轉了轉裡頭的機率螺絲，祈禱沒人看到。

column1: Good idea! You smash the video blackjack machine with your hammer, causing hundreds of dollars to spill out, which you gladly catch and put in your pockets.
column2: 好主意！你掄起槌子把電子二十一點機砸爛，成堆的鈔票嘩啦啦掉出來，你笑呵呵地全接進口袋。

column1: Bad idea! The security guard handles you indelicately as he escorts you to the local jail!
column2: 爛主意！保全毫不客氣地把你「請」進了當地的監獄！

column1: ...maybe with good behavior?
column2: ……搞不好，表現良好還能假釋出獄？

column1: Next time, Larry, be a little more careful with the equipment!
column2: Larry，下次對機器溫柔一點啦，別又用蠻力！

column1: Congratulations, Larry! You've broken the house limit.
column2: 恭喜啊，Larry！你把賭場的天花板都掀了！

column1: Please wait until this hand is over.
column2: 請等這局結束。

column1: You bet zero dollars, receive zero cards, and win zero dollars and zero cents.
column2: 你押零元、拿零張牌、贏零元零角——穩賺不賠的空氣局。

column1: You can play all night like this!
column2: 照這樣玩，你可以耗一整夜還原地踏步！

column1: You can't bet more than you have.
column2: 你身上沒那麼多錢，別亂下注啦。

column1: You have Blackjack!
column2: 你拿到二十一點啦！

column1: Unfortunately, so does the dealer, so this hand is a push.
column2: 不巧莊家也是二十一點，這局算平手。

column1: The dealer has Blackjack!
column2: 莊家二十一點！

column1: Your first hand has Blackjack!
column2: 你的第一手是二十一點！

column1: Your split hand has Blackjack!
column2: 你分牌那手也是二十一點！

column1: First, click on Deal to start a new hand.
column2: 先點一下「發牌」開始新的一局。

column1: You busted.
column2: 你爆啦。

column1: You busted!
column2: 爆牌啦！

column1: Your first hand busted.
column2: 你的第一手爆了。

column1: You have six cards whose total is not over 21!
column2: 你湊了六張牌，總點數還沒超過二十一——過關！

column1: Your split hand busted!
column2: 你分牌那手爆啦！

column1: Your split hand has six cards!
column2: 你分牌那手湊滿六張牌了！

column1: The dealer busted!
column2: 莊家爆啦！

column1: It's a push.
column2: 平手。

column1: Your first hand wins!

column2: 不、不、不！「雨鞋」可不是這樣用的啦！

column1: Your wallet won't fit in the candy box.
column2: 你的錢包塞不進糖果盒。

column1: The disco pass is already paid for.
column2: 迪斯可通行證已經付過錢了。

column1: The pocket knife seems unimpressed with your wallet.
column2: 小刀對你的錢包一點興趣都沒有。

column1: You foolishly dip your wallet into the wine.
column2: 你蠢蛋似地把錢包泡進酒裡。

column1: The girls in the magazine are mightily impressed with your manly, bulging wallet.
column2: 雜誌上的女孩們，對你那鼓鼓的男人味錢包，佩服得五體投地。

column1: There's no need to put the wallet on the hammer.
column2: 沒必要把錢包放在錘子上。

column1: Try as you might, you can't stuff the wallet into the pill container.
column2: 任你再怎麼塞，錢包就是塞不進藥瓶。

column1: You show the wallet to the ribbon, but (strange but true) it doesn't respond.
column2: 你把錢包秀給緞帶看，但（說也奇怪）它沒反應。

column1: You place the graffiti in your wallet (but it still shows up here!).
column2: 你把塗鴉塞進錢包（但它還是會顯示在這裡！）。

column1: Don't waste precious breath spray on your wallet.
column2: 別把珍貴的口氣噴霧浪費在錢包上。

column1: Yep. It's your breath spray.
column2: 沒錯，這就是你的口氣噴霧。

column1: A little breath spray makes even the cheapest watch shine like gold (plate).
column2: 噴點口氣噴霧，連最廉價的手錶都能閃得像鍍金一樣。

column1: The apple was previously well-sprayed with an assortment of interesting chemical compounds.
column2: 這顆蘋果之前已經被各種有趣的化學藥劑噴得夠徹底了。

column1: That ought to kill the taste of Lefty's sink!
column2: 這樣應該能蓋掉老左酒吧那水槽的怪味了！

column1: No amount of breath spray will help the taste of this stuff.
column2: 再多口氣噴霧也救不了這玩意兒的味道。

column1: The remote control's keys are sticky enough already.
column2: 遙控器的按鈕已經夠黏的了。

column1: The rose smells good enough already.
column2: 玫瑰已經夠香了。

column1: Your breath spray will clash with that natural minty taste of latex.
column2: 你的口氣噴霧，會跟那乳膠製品天然的薄荷味互相衝突。

column1: The candy smells great just the way it is.
column2: 糖果本身的味道已經夠讚了。

column1: Perhaps you should use the breath spray AT the disco instead!
column2: 也許你該留著口氣噴霧，到迪斯可現場再用吧！

column1: It's pointless to freshen your knife's breath, let alone its width!
column2: 幫小刀「清新口氣」根本沒意義，更別提它的「寬度」了！

column1: Mmm, Chablis with a hint of wintergreen! You may have something there.
column2: 嗯～夏布利白酒帶點冬青薄荷香，你可能歪打正著了。

column1: Better not, the pages are sticky enough already.
column2: 還是別了，書頁已經夠黏的了。

column1: Now the hammer is minty fresh.
column2: 現在錘子聞起來清涼薄荷味十足。

column1: That would ruin the wonderful medicinal flavor of the pills.
column2: 那樣會毀了藥丸那美妙的藥味。

column1: Yeah, great idea.
column2: 是喔，好主意。

column1: You'd better not; you don't want to smear your writing.
column2: 還是別了；你不會想把你寫的字弄糊掉。

column1: Your watch won't fit in your already bulging wallet.
column2: 你的手錶塞不進已經鼓鼓的錢包。

column1: Your breath spray couldn't care less what time it is.
column2: 你的口氣噴霧根本懶得管現在幾點。

column1: Pretty clever! "Watch the apple!!"
column2: 有梗喔！這算是「手錶站崗顧蘋果」吧！！

column1: The expensive ring, seeing your cheap watch, exudes a strong sense of superiority.
column2: 那只名貴的戒指，看到你那支廉價手錶，整個散發出高人一等的優越感。

column1: You dip your watch in the whiskey, then give it a licking, yet it keeps on ticking!
column2: 你把手錶浸進威士忌，還舔了一口——不愧是「愈操愈耐操」的錶，還在滴答滴答！

column1: Is there something good to "watch" on TV, Larry?
column2: 電視上有什麼好「看」的嗎，賴瑞？

column1: So you want to time the rose?
column2: 所以你想幫玫瑰計時喔？

column1: That should make your cheap watch waterproof!
column2: 這樣應該能讓你那支廉價手錶防水了！

column1: Hiding your watch in the box of candy won't get you anywhere.
column2: 把手錶藏進糖果盒，也不會有什麼用。

column1: The pass couldn't be less interested.
column2: 通行證完全沒興趣。

column1: To a pocketknife, time is relative.
column2: 對一把小刀來說，時間是相對的。

column1: You idiotically dip your watch into the wine. How much punishment can these things take?
column2: 你像白痴一樣把手錶泡進酒裡。這玩意兒到底能撐多少折騰啊？

column1: Almost time to buy the next issue!
column2: 差不多該買下一期了！

column1: The hammer doesn't want to watch.
column2: 錘子沒興趣「看」。

column1: The watch won't fit into the little pill container.
column2: 手錶塞不進那個小藥瓶。

column1: You attempt to measure the length of the ribbon with your watch, and find it's about 30 seconds long!
column2: 你想用手錶量緞帶的長度，結果量出「30 秒長」！

column1: What a stupid thing to try.
column2: 這招也太蠢了吧。

column1: The apple won't fit in your already-bulging wallet.
column2: 蘋果塞不進你已經鼓鼓的錢包。

column1: Warning: do not try to puncture the breath spray with an apple.
column2: 「警告：請勿嘗試用蘋果戳破口氣噴霧罐。」

column1: The watch concentrates on keeping time and ignores your apple.
column2: 手錶一心一意在計時，懶得理你的蘋果。

column1: This Red Delicious is not suitable for your Granny Smith, but someone else might find it appropriate.
column2: 這顆五爪蘋果跟你的青蘋果不搭嘎，不過別人可能覺得挺速配的。

column1: No, the apple doesn't fit through the ring.
column2: 不行，蘋果穿不過戒指。

column1: You fruitlessly attempt to wedge the apple into the whiskey glass.
column2: 你徒勞無功（果然很「沒果」）地想把蘋果塞進威士忌杯。

column1: Having no infrared circuitry, the apple fails to interface with the remote control.
column2: 蘋果沒有紅外線電路，沒辦法跟遙控器搭上線。

column1: The apple and the rose, two of nature's most perfect creations. So what?
column2: 蘋果和玫瑰，大自然最完美的兩件傑作。那又怎樣？

column1: The apple already has a protective skin. Don't bite either one!
column2: 蘋果本身就有保護皮了。這兩個都別咬！

column1: The apple won't fit in the candy box.
column2: 蘋果塞不進糖果盒。

column1: The disco pass prefers saltier snacks.
column2: 迪斯可通行證比較愛鹹的零食。

column1: There's no need to peel the apple.
column2: 沒必要削蘋果皮。

column1: Larry, it's pears in tequila bottles, not apples in wine boxes.
column2: 賴瑞，龍舌蘭酒瓶裡泡的是梨子，不是葡萄酒盒裡塞蘋果啦。

column1: Seeing the melons on the front of that magazine, the apple develops an inferiority complex.
column2: 看到雜誌封面上那對「哈密瓜」，蘋果頓時自卑了起來。

column1: Applesauce. Good idea.
column2: 蘋果醬啊，好主意。

column1: You try to core the apple with the pill bottle, but are unable to remove the child safety lid!
column2: 你想用藥瓶挖掉蘋果核，但打不開兒童安全蓋！

column1: Tie a pink ribbon round your old apple... Nah. That just doesn't make it, Larry.
column2: 「幫你的老蘋果繫條粉紅緞帶…」不行啦，這樣不行，賴瑞。

column1: The apple is shocked and confused by the strange graffiti.
column2: 蘋果被這詭異的塗鴉嚇得一頭霧水。

column1: The ring might puncture the thin vinylette of your El Cheapo wallet.
column2: The ring might puncture the thin vinylette of your El Cheapo wallet.

column1: The breathspray looks at the ring in surprise! "Why, this is so sudden!"
column2: 口氣噴霧驚訝地看著戒指！「哎呀，這也太突然了吧！」

column1: If clocks chime, do watches "ring?"
column2: 時鐘敲鐘會噹噹響，那手錶戴上戒指，該不會也想叮一聲吧？

column1: Putting the ring in the apple might spoil its lustrous shine! (The apple's, that is!)
column2: 把戒指放進蘋果，可能會毀了它那閃亮的光澤！（是說蘋果的啦！）

column1: When you gaze deep into the diamond, you seem to hear the sound of wedding bells, and maybe diesel trucks.
column2: 你凝視鑽石深處，彷彿聽見婚禮鐘聲——搞不好還有柴油卡車的引擎聲。

column1: Checking for dissolvable solids, you dip the ring into the whiskey. Nothing happens.
column2: 為了檢查有沒有可溶固體，你把戒指泡進威士忌。什麼事都沒發生。

column1: Just for fun, you use the ring to press some buttons on the remote control.
column2: 純粹好玩，你用戒指按了幾下遙控器的按鈕。

column1: It's no use, the rose is already engaged to some pansy.
column2: 沒用的，玫瑰早就跟某朵「三色堇」訂婚了。

column1: That's a creative way to give someone a ring.
column2: 這方法還真有創意，這樣「求婚」喔。

column1: You smear chocolate all over the ring, then lick it all off. Was that fun or what?
column2: 你把巧克力抹滿整只戒指，然後全部舔乾淨。爽吧，是不是？

column1: The disco pass has no use for the ring.
column2: 迪斯可通行證對戒指沒轍。

column1: You rub the diamond on the blade of your pocket knife and sharpen it ever so slightly.
column2: 你用鑽石磨小刀的刀刃，稍微磨利了一點點。

column1: Using the diamond, you scratch your initials into the cardboard box of wine.
column2: 你用鑽石在酒盒的紙箱上刻下自己的名字縮寫。

column1: These are only photographs of girls, Larry. None of them will marry you.
column2: 這些不過是女孩的照片，賴瑞。沒有一個會嫁給你的。

column1: The ring won't fit on the hammer.
column2: 戒指套不進錘子。

column1: Don't put the ring in with the pills. You might accidentally swallow it.
column2: 別把戒指跟藥丸放一起，你可能不小心吞下去。

column1: Your ribbon WOULD look sweet with a cute little bow, but you decide to save the ribbon to use elsewhere.
column2: 你的緞帶打個可愛蝴蝶結是真的會很好看，但你決定留著它，之後另有用途。

column1: The ring read the graffiti while it was sitting on the sink.
column2: 戒指放在水槽上時，順便看了一下那塗鴉。

column1: Your wallet is stained enough already.
column2: 你的錢包已經夠髒的了。

column1: You don't really want "Morning in the Distillery" breath?
column2: 你該不會真想要一口「酒廠清晨」味的口氣吧？

column1: You drop a little whiskey on your watch, just to clean the crystal.
column2: 你滴了點威士忌在手錶上，純粹想擦亮錶面水晶。

column1: Is whiskey and apples an old family recipe?
column2: 威士忌配蘋果，是你家的傳家秘方喔？

column1: You dribble a bit of whiskey onto the ring, hoping to kill the germs from the bathroom sink.
column2: 你滴了點威士忌在戒指上，希望能殺死浴室水槽上沾到的細菌。

column1: There is a strong odor of petroleum by-products coming from this glass.
column2: 這杯子散發出一股濃烈的石化製品味道。

column1: I'll bet a little whiskey would make that remote control more powerful, you conjecture mistakenly.
column2: 「我賭一點威士忌，能讓這遙控器功力大增，」你異想天開地猜測。

column1: If the rose isn't already dead, that rotgut will put it out of its misery.
column2: 如果玫瑰還沒死透，這杯劣酒絕對能讓它一了百了。

column1: Nobody's going to want to drink the whiskey after you've poured it in THERE!
column2: 你倒進「那個地方」以後，沒人還會想喝這杯威士忌了！

column1: The candy is already well-soaked.
column2: 糖果已經夠濕透的了。

column1: You dribble a little whiskey on the pass to remove that "never used" sheen.
column2: 你滴了點威士忌在通行證上，想去掉那股「從沒用過」的新品光澤。

column1: Don't you know? Whiskey and weapons don't mix!
column2: 你不知道嗎？威士忌跟武器可不能混在一起！

column1: Yes, a good malt Chablis is tough to find.
column2: 沒錯，一支好喝的「麥芽夏布利」還真是可遇不可求。

column1: That will only make the pages even more sticky.
column2: 那只會讓書頁更黏而已。

column1: The whiskey is all you need to get hammered!
column2: 威士忌就夠讓你醉到掛啦！

column1: A Mickey Finn will not get you anywhere.
column2: 下迷魂藥也是白忙一場。

column1: Great, now you've got a whiskey-soiled ribbon!
column2: 讚喔，你那條絲帶現在泡出一身酒味了！

column1: You slop a little whiskey onto your carefully-scrawled graffiti.
column2: 你把威士忌潑到自己嘔心瀝血塗的鴉上，糗！

column1: Infrared doesn't work on leather.
column2: 紅外線對皮革沒路用。

column1: You try to change spray channels with the remote control, but it doesn't work.
column2: 你想用遙控器切噴霧頻道，轉半天轉不動。

column1: You are surprised to see the watch change to another time zone, then revert back to local, Lost Wages time again!
column2: 你嚇一跳，手錶居然跳到別的時區，一晃眼又跳回失樂園當地時間！

column1: You attempt to change the apple into something else with the remote control, but nothing happens.
column2: 你想用遙控器把蘋果變成別的東西，結果什麼鳥事都沒發生。

column1: The remote control causes no change in the ring.
column2: 遙控器對戒指一點屁用都沒有。

column1: You DO wish the whiskey would change to a better brand!
column2: 你多希望遙控器能把這廉價威士忌變成正牌貨啊！

column1: Operating on the principle of line-of-sight infrared transmission, the remote control baffles you completely.
column2: 這遙控器靠的是直線紅外線傳輸原理，聽起來就讓你一頭霧水。

column1: You watch closely to see if the remote control affects the rose, but it doesn't.
column2: 你死盯著看遙控器對玫瑰有沒有反應，結果沒有。

column1: You try to "turn on" the lubber with your remote.
column2: 你異想天開想用遙控器"撩"一下你的套子。

column1: The box of candy doesn't react to the remote control.
column2: 巧克力盒對遙控器沒反應。

column1: Using the remote control on the disco pass is futile.
column2: 拿遙控器對付舞廳通行證，根本沒用。

column1: You try to change blades on the pocketknife but naturally, nothing happens!
column2: 你想換小刀的刀片，當然啦，什麼都沒發生！

column1: Using the remote, you attempt to improve the quality of the wine, but nothing happens.
column2: 你想用遙控器讓這酒變高級一點，結果沒用。

column1: Remote controls often fail to work with the print medium, Larry.
column2: 遙控器對印刷品通常沒轍的，賴瑞。

column1: The remote control curiously has no effect on the hammer.
column2: 說也奇怪，遙控器對錘子完全沒作用。

column1: You pointlessly click the remote control at the pills.
column2: 你白費力氣對著藥丸按遙控器。

column1: The remote control has no effect on the ribbon.
column2: 遙控器對絲帶沒轍。

column1: The graffiti doesn't become any more interesting when you click the remote control on it.
column2: 對著塗鴉猛按遙控器，它還是一樣無聊。

column1: You're such an incurable romantic! Pressing the rose in your wallet would be sweet, but might well ruin it!
column2: 你真是無藥可救的浪漫鬼！把玫瑰壓進皮夾是很甜蜜啦，但搞不好會壓爛它！

column1: Take that, you cry, spanking the breath spray with the stem of your rose!
column2: 吃我這招！你大喊，用玫瑰莖打口氣噴霧的屁股！

column1: The rose makes a lovely gift, but the watch isn't interested.
column2: 玫瑰是很浪漫的禮物，可惜手錶沒興趣。

column1: You gently the rub the tender, red, slightly-opened petals of the rose on the firm, rounded cheeks of the apple.
column2: 你溫柔地拿玫瑰嬌嫩微綻的紅花瓣，摩挲蘋果那圓潤結實的「臉蛋」。

column1: You thread the rose through the ring, which does nothing for the ring, but it does make the rose feel quite special.
column2: 你把玫瑰穿過戒指，戒指沒感覺，玫瑰倒是爽得很。

column1: Putting the rose in that rotgut will kill the poor rose.
column2: 把玫瑰泡進那杯劣酒，會要了這可憐花的命。

column1: The remote control isn't remotely interested in the rose.
column2: 那遙控器對玫瑰一點都不「遙」感興趣。

column1: A rose, is a rose, is a rose.
column2: 玫瑰就是玫瑰，就是玫瑰。

column1: Ever hear of thorns, Larry? You're going to luin your lubber!
column2: 沒聽過玫瑰有刺嗎，賴瑞？你這樣會把套子給戳爆！

column1: Rose and candy DO go together, but not now.
column2: 玫瑰配巧克力確實是絕配，但不是現在。

column1: Great idea, Larry.
column2: 好主意，賴瑞。

column1: Got it! You slice off a thorn from the stem of the rose.
column2: 搞定！你把玫瑰莖上的刺削掉了。

column1: That cheap wine will only give the rose a headache.
column2: 那廉價酒只會讓玫瑰頭痛。

column1: You could press the rose in an encyclopedia, but not in a magazine.
column2: 玫瑰可以夾進百科全書，但夾雜誌不行。

column1: The rose is too delicate to do anything with the hammer.
column2: 玫瑰太嬌貴了，錘子碰不得。

column1: error1!
column2: error1!

column1: Huh?
column2: 啥？

column1: Yes, that's the traditional place to keep lubbers, Larry, but you've got more than enough room right here.
column2: 沒錯，那是放套子的老地方，賴瑞，不過你這裡空間多得是。

column1: Your lubber already has a delightful aroma all its own.
column2: 你的套子本來就自帶香氣，不用加工。

column1: Save the lubber for a higher purpose, Larry.
column2: 那套子留著幹大事吧，賴瑞。

column1: The apple already has a protective skin!
column2: 蘋果本來就自帶防護皮啦！

column1: Putting the lubber over the ring is a waste of latex.
column2: 把套子套在戒指上，根本是浪費橡膠。

column1: Tsk, tsk! You should have bought the lubricated lubbers?
column2: 嘖嘖！早跟你說要買潤滑款的套子了吧？

column1: Placing the lubber over the remote control will interfere with its emissions! (Of infrared light, Larry!)
column2: 把套子套在遙控器上會干擾它的發射！（是紅外線啦，賴瑞，別想歪！）

column1: The rose's thorns would adversely affect the structural integrity of your lubber.
column2: 玫瑰的刺會嚴重破壞你套子的「結構強度」。

column1: These are more fun to use than to look at!
column2: 這東西用的比看的爽多了！

column1: Among others, the candy prefers to come in an attractive box.
column2: 說到底，巧克力還是待在漂亮的盒子裡最對味。

column1: The disco pass has already been laminated for your protection.
column2: 舞廳通行證早就層壓好了，保護周全。

column1: That's one pointy object that shouldn't be brought into contact with your lubber!
column2: 那尖尖的東西，千萬別讓它碰到你的套子！

column1: The wine comes in a handy, professional-series, cardboard box.
column2: 這酒裝在方便攜帶的專業紙盒裡。

column1: You can bet the ladies in that magazine have their own birth control devices.
column2: 敢說那本雜誌裡的妹子個個都有自己的避孕小物。

column1: Right idea. Wrong tool.
column2: 想法沒錯，工具不對。

column1: Putting the pills in the lubber will only cause you great irritation later on.
column2: 把藥丸塞進套子，只會害你之後爽不起來。
